items.representative_example: This sentence has not been processed yet.

1.label: sentence
1.text: This sentence has not been processed yet.
1.description: Fallback component for sentences that do not match any defined template.
examples: This sentence has not been processed yet.

items.representative_example: Micro-organisms that may present a health risk shall be eliminated.

1.label: SubjectNounPhrase
1.description: Entity or agents identified as potentially presenting a risk.

2.label: RiskVerbPhrase
2.description: Verb phrase expressing potential or conditional risk.

3.label: RequiredAction
3.description: Mandatory action required to mitigate or eliminate the identified risk.

items.representative_example: Where feasible, protective clothing shall be worn.

1.label: Condition
1.description: Contextual or situational condition that limits or qualifies the obligation.

2.label: MainAttestation
2.description: Main regulatory statement expressing an obligation, recommendation, or requirement.

items.representative_example: If the pH falls below 4.2, the batch must be discarded.

1.label: Parameter
1.description: Measurable parameter being monitored.

2.label: Limit
2.description: Numeric threshold that triggers the action (value + optional unit).

3.label: Action
3.description: Required action once the threshold is crossed.

items.representative_example: Unless refrigerated, cooked meat shall not be stored longer than 2 hours.

1.label: ExceptionCondition
1.description: Condition under which the main attestation does NOT apply (exception).

2.label: Attestation
2.text: cooked meat shall not be stored longer than 2 hours
2.description: Main regulatory statement that applies when the exception condition is NOT met.
examples: cooked meat shall not be stored longer than 2 hours
the product shall not be distributed

items.communicative_function: Allow an action “provided that” a safeguard is met
items.representative_example: Packaging may be reused provided that it is thoroughly cleaned and sanitized.

1.label: PermittedAction
1.description: Entity or action that is being permitted under a safeguard condition.

2.label: BaseVerbPhrase
2.description: Verb phrase expressing the permitted action or state.

3.label: SafeguardCondition
3.text: it is thoroughly cleaned and sanitized
3.description: Safeguard requirement that must be met for the permission to apply.
examples: it is thoroughly cleaned and sanitized

items.representative_example: Products shall be packaged promptly.

1.label: Subject
1.description: Entity or item to which the obligation applies.

2.label: PastParticiple
2.description: Past participle expressing the mandated action or state (passive voice).

3.label: Complement
3.description: Optional complement specifying manner, place, timing, or condition.

items.communicative_function: Require compliance with a standard, limit, or criterion
items.representative_example: Products shall comply with the maximum residue limits established by the Codex Alimentarius Commission.

1.label: Subject
1.description: Entity that must meet the referenced standard, limit, or criterion.

2.label: StandardOrCriterion
2.text: the maximum residue limits established by the Codex Alimentarius Commission
2.description: The referenced standard, limit, criterion, or requirement that must be complied with.
examples: the maximum residue limits established by the Codex Alimentarius Commission

items.representative_example: Precautions shall be taken to prevent contamination from moisture.

1.label: PrecautionOrMeasure
1.description: Noun phrase naming the precaution(s) or measure(s) that must be taken.

2.label: PurposeInfinitive
2.description: Infinitive verb phrase stating the purpose of the precautionary measures.
examples: reduce the risk of microbial growth

items.representative_example: No raw material shall be accepted if known to contain toxic substances.

1.label: Quantifier
1.description: Quantifier establishing acceptance or rejection scope.

2.label: Material
2.description: Material, input, or item subject to acceptance or rejection.

3.label: PastParticiple
3.description: Past participle expressing acceptance or rejection decision.

4.label: Condition
4.description: Condition that triggers acceptance or rejection.

items.representative_example: Competent authorities must implement a water-safety management plan in accordance with current regulations.

1.label: Agent
1.description: Responsible entity (authority, operator, establishment).

2.label: BaseVerb
2.description: Base verb expressing the mandated action.

3.label: ObjectOrProcess
3.description: Object, plan, procedure, or process required by the rule.

items.representative_example: Natural flavoring substances may be used in products covered by this Standard.

1.label: SubstanceOrAction
1.description: Substance or action that is explicitly permitted.

2.label: PastParticiple
2.description: Past participle expressing the permitted action or state.

3.label: ProductOrCategory
3.description: Product, food category, or scope to which the permission applies.

4.label: Standard
4.description: Referenced standard or regulation governing the permitted use.

items.representative_example: Permitted ingredients: starter cultures of harmless microorganisms; sodium chloride; potable water.

1.label: ItemCategory
1.description: Category or class of items that are explicitly permitted.

2.label: PermittedItemList
2.text: starter cultures of harmless microorganisms; sodium chloride; potable water
2.description: Explicit list of permitted items, typically separated by semicolons or commas.
examples: starter cultures of harmless microorganisms; sodium chloride; potable water
citric acid; ascorbic acid; lactic acid

items.representative_example: Allergenic ingredients may be added only if cross-contact is prevented.

1.label: SubstanceOrAction
1.description: Substance or action that is permitted under a specific condition.

2.label: BaseVerb
2.description: Base verb describing the permitted action.

3.label: Condition
3.description: Condition that must be met for the permission to apply.

items.representative_example: Except for use in salted fish at up to 200 mg/kg, sodium benzoate may be used.

1.label: Context
1.description: Specific context, product, use, or situation that defines the exception scope.

2.label: SubstanceOrAction
2.description: Substance or action to which the permission applies under the stated exception.

3.label: PastParticiple
3.description: Past participle expressing the permitted action or state.

4.label: LimitOrDetails
4.description: Optional limits, conditions, or additional details applicable within the exception.

items.communicative_function: Authorize an action through a responsible agent
items.representative_example: The competent authority may approve recirculated water for food processing.

1.label: Authority
1.description: Competent authority or responsible body empowered to grant authorization.

2.label: BaseVerb
2.description: Base verb expressing the act of authorization or approval.

3.label: ProcessOrMaterial
3.description: Process, practice, or material being authorized.

4.label: ReferenceOrGoal
4.description: Optional reference to standards, regulations, or the purpose of the authorization.

items.representative_example: The product shall not be treated with ionizing irradiation.

1.label: Subject
1.description: Entity or product to which the prohibition applies.

2.label: PastParticiple
2.description: Past participle expressing the prohibited treatment or condition.

3.label: AgentOrMethod
3.description: Optional agent, method, or technology that is explicitly prohibited.

items.communicative_function: Ban a substance or exceeding a limit
items.representative_example: The product shall not contain histamine above 20 mg/kg.

1.label: Subject
1.description: Entity being regulated.

2.label: SubstanceOrValueUnit
2.description: Substance being prohibited or parameter/value being constrained.

3.label: Limit
3.description: Optional threshold level or comparative limit (value + unit).

items.representative_example: Containers must not be placed directly on the floor.

1.label: Item
1.description: Item, material, or object subject to the prohibition.

2.label: Preposition
2.description: Preposition expressing spatial or relational restriction.

3.label: ObjectOrLocation
3.description: Object, surface, location, or item with which association or placement is prohibited.

items.representative_example: Use of wood should be avoided unless it can be thoroughly cleaned and disinfected.

1.label: ActionOrMaterial
1.description: Action, material, or practice that is discouraged or prohibited by default.

2.label: RiskEliminatingCondition
2.text: it can be thoroughly cleaned and disinfected
2.description: Condition that eliminates or sufficiently mitigates the risk, allowing an exception.
examples: it can be thoroughly cleaned and disinfected

items.communicative_function: Exclude risky persons, practices, or materials
items.representative_example: No raw material shall be accepted if known to contain parasites.

1.label: Quantifier
1.description: Quantifier defining the scope of exclusion.

2.label: Noun
2.description: Person, practice, material, lot, or entity subject to exclusion.

3.label: VerbForm
3.description: Verb phrase describing what is prohibited (active form or passive form).

items.communicative_function: Cite an official limit or condition
items.representative_example: Limits for pesticides established by the Codex Alimentarius Commission shall apply.

1.label: Parameter
1.description: Official limit, condition, or criterion being cited.

2.label: Authority
2.description: Authority or body that established or approved the limit or condition.

3.label: ProductOrProcess
3.description: Product, category, or process to which the limit or condition applies.

items.representative_example: A lot shall be considered acceptable when the number of defectives does not exceed the acceptance number of the sampling plan (AQL 6.5).
items.structural_template: *<Subject> *[shall/must/should] be considered acceptable *[when/if] *<Criterion> does not exceed *<LimitOrNumber>.

1.label: Subject
1.description: Entity, lot, or unit whose acceptability is being assessed.

2.label: Criterion
2.description: Measured criterion used to assess acceptability.

3.label: LimitOrNumber
3.text: the acceptance number of the sampling plan (AQL 6.5)
3.description: Quantitative limit, acceptance number, or threshold defining acceptability.
examples: the acceptance number of the sampling plan (AQL 6.5)

items.communicative_function: Require absence of an undesirable condition
items.representative_example: The product shall be free from abnormal external moisture, excluding condensation after cold storage.

1.label: Product
1.description: Product, material, or item to which the condition applies.

2.label: UndesirableCondition
2.description: Undesirable condition, defect, or characteristic that must be absent.

3.label: AllowedCircumstance
3.description: Specific circumstance under which the undesirable condition is allowed or tolerated.

items.representative_example: The process shall achieve at least a 5-log reduction of Salmonella.
items.structural_template: *<Subject> *[shall/must/should] achieve *[at least/no less than/not less than/not below] *<PerformanceMetric> of *<Target>.

1.label: Subject
1.description: Process, system, or operational step that is required to achieve the specified performance target.

2.label: PerformanceMetric
2.description: Quantitative performance level that must be achieved, usually expressed as a reduction, inactivation, or effectiveness measure.

3.label: Target
3.description: Hazard, organism, or parameter against which the performance metric is measured.

items.representative_example: Products shall be inspected by a competent person before distribution.

1.label: ProductOrProcess
1.description: Product, process, or activity subject to inspection, testing, or monitoring.

2.label: Agent
2.description: Person, authority, or body responsible for carrying out the inspection, testing, or monitoring.

3.label: Frequency
3.description: Timing or frequency at which the inspection, testing, or monitoring is performed.

4.label: Purpose
4.description: Intended purpose or objective of the inspection, testing, or monitoring activity.
